-module(solver).
-export([solve/2]).

%% @doc Solves the problem: counting connected induced subgraphs with an even sum of node values.
%% @spec solve([integer()], [[integer()]]) -> integer()
solve(Nums, Edges) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    Adj = build_adjacency_list(N, Edges),
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate through all masks from 1 to 2^N - 1
    AllMasks = 1 .. (1 div (1 end) * (2^N - 1)),
    
    fun process_mask(Mask) ->
        % 1. Check the sum of node values (Parity check)
        Sum = calculate_sum(Nums, Mask, N),
        
        if Sum rem 2 /= 0 ->
            0; % Sum is odd, skip
        true ->
            % 2. Check if the induced subgraph is connected
            SubsetNodes = get_subset_nodes(Mask, N),
            
            if SubsetNodes = [] ->
                0; % Should not happen for Mask >= 1
            true ->
                IsConnected = check_connectivity(Mask, SubsetNodes, Adj, N),
                if IsConnected ->
                    1;
                true ->
                    0
                end
            end
        end
    end,
    
    TotalCount = lists:sum([process_mask(M) || M <- AllMasks]),
    TotalCount.

%% Helper to build the adjacency list representation of the graph
build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    %% Initialize N empty lists
    Adj_init = lists:replicate(N, []),
    
    lists:foldl(fun(Adj, [U, V]) ->
        Adj1 = lists:insert_at(V, U, Adj),
        Adj2 = lists:insert_at(U, V, Adj1)
    end, Adj_init, Edges).

%% Helper to calculate the sum of values in the subset represented by Mask
calculate_sum(Nums, Mask, N) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Sum, I) ->
        if (Mask div (2^I)) rem 2 =:= 1 ->
            Sum + Nums !! I
        ;
            Sum
        end
    end, 0, 0 .. N-1).

%% Helper to get the list of node indices present in the subset
get_subset_nodes(Mask, N) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Nodes, I) ->
        if (Mask div (2^I)) rem 2 =:= 1 ->
            [I | Nodes]
        ;
            Nodes
        end
    end, [], 0 .. N-1).

%% Helper to check connectivity using BFS on the induced subgraph
check_connectivity(Mask, SubsetNodes, Adj, N) ->
    case SubsetNodes of
        [] -> false;
        [StartNode|_] ->
            % BFS implementation
            BFS(Mask, Adj, StartNode, SubsetNodes).

%% BFS implementation to check if all nodes in the subset are reachable from the start node
BFS(Mask, Adj, StartNode, SubsetNodes) ->
    % Queue initialized with start node
    Queue = [StartNode],
    % Visited set (represented by a list/boolean array, since N <= 13, a boolean array is fine)
    Visited_Array = lists:replicate(N, false),
    
    % Mark start node as visited
    Visited_Array_Start = lists:replace(Visited_Array, [StartNode], true),
    
    % State: {Queue, VisitedArray, VisitedCount}
    State = {Queue, Visited_Array_Start, 1},
    
    % Run BFS loop
    {FinalQueue, FinalVisited, FinalCount} = loop(State, Mask, Adj, SubsetNodes),
    
    % Connected if the count equals the size of the subset
    FinalCount == length(SubsetNodes).

loop({Queue, Visited, Count}, Mask, Adj, SubsetNodes) ->
    case Queue of
        [] -> {Queue, Visited, Count}; % Queue is empty, BFS finished
        [U | TQueue] ->
            
            % Process neighbors of U
            Neighbors = Adj !! U,
            
            {NewQueue, NewVisited, NewCount} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) ->
                
                % Check if V is in the subset AND not visited
                if (Mask div (2^V)) rem 2 =:= 1 and not lists:nth(V, Acc#Visited, true) ->
                    Acc#Visited = lists:replace(Acc#Visited, [V], true),
                    Acc#Queue = [V | Acc#Queue],
                    {Acc#Queue, Acc#Visited, Acc#Count + 1}
                ;
                    Acc
                end
            end, {TQueue, Visited, Count}, Neighbors),
            
            loop({NewQueue, NewVisited, NewCount}, Mask, Adj, SubsetNodes)
    end.

{-
Note: In a real performance-critical scenario, one might use an external library or more complex state passing.
Given N<=13, this list manipulation approach is acceptable for correctness demonstration.

as the previous structure was overly complicated for simple recursion.
-}

check_connectivity_simplified(Mask, SubsetNodes, Adj, N) ->
    case SubsetNodes of
        [] -> false;
        [StartNode|_] ->
            BFS_Simple(Mask, Adj, StartNode, SubsetNodes).

BFS_Simple(Mask, Adj, StartNode, SubsetNodes) ->
    % Queue: [Nodes to visit]
    Queue = [StartNode],
    % Visited: Array/list tracking visited status within the subset
    Visited = lists:replace(lists:replicate(N, false), [StartNode], true),
    
    % Recursive helper: {Queue, Visited, Count}
    bfs_loop(Queue, Visited, 1, SubsetNodes).

bfs_loop(Queue, Visited, Count, SubsetNodes) ->

            all_masks(Mask + 1, MaxMask, N, Nums, Adj, Count)

solve(Nums, Edges) ->

    Count.

build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    lists:foldl(fn(Adj, [U, V]) ->
        lists:replace(Adj, U, lists:insert_at(V, lists:replace(lists:nth(U, Adj), []))),
        lists:replace(Adj, V, lists:insert_at(U, lists:replace(lists:nth(V, Adj), [])))
    end,
    lists:replicate(N, []),
    Edges).

%% Helper function to iterate through all subsets and check conditions.
count_valid_subsets(N, Nums, Adj) ->
    TotalMasks = 120*120, % Max 2^13 - 1 = 8191. This is a placeholder, we iterate up to 2^N - 1

        if Mask =:= 0 -> Acc;
        true ->
            % Check mask: 1 to 2^N - 1
            Sum = check_subset(N, Nums, Adj, Mask),
            if is_integer(Sum) ->
                Acc + Sum;
                Acc
            end
        end
    end, 0, lists:seq(1, 2^N - 1)),

check_subset(N, Nums, Adj, Mask) ->
    % 1. Check sum parity
    Sum = sum_of_subset_values(N, Nums, Mask),
    if Sum rem 2 /= 0 ->
        0;
        true ->
            % 2. Check connectivity
            if is_connected(N, Adj, Mask) ->
                1;
                0
            end
        end.

%% Calculates the sum of values for nodes in the subset represented by Mask.
sum_of_subset_values(N, Nums, Mask) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        if (Mask div (2^I)) rem 2 == 1 ->
            Acc + Nums[I];
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, lists:seq(0, N - 1)).

is_connected(N, Adj, Mask) ->
    % Find the first node in the subset

        StartNode = StartNode + 1
    end,

-module(solver_module).

solve(Nums, Edges) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    Adj = build_adjacency_list(N, Edges),
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate through all masks from 1 to 2^N - 1
    MaxMask = 1 endof(2, N),
    
    do {
        Mask = Mask + 1,
        if Mask > MaxMask ->
            break
        end;
        
        % 1. Check the sum of node values (Parity check)
        {Sum, SubsetNodes} = calculate_subset_info(Nums, N, Mask),
        
        if Sum rem 2 == 0 ->
            % Sum is even, check connectivity
            IsConnected = check_connectivity(Adj, Mask, SubsetNodes, N),
            
            if IsConnected ->
                Count = Count + 1
            end;
        end;
    }, Mask = 1, Count = 0.

build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    % Initialize adjacency list: a list of N empty lists
    Adj = lists:replicate(N, []),
    
    % Fold over edges to build the adjacency list
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, [U, V]) ->
        % Add V to U's neighbors and U to V's neighbors
        NewAcc = lists:update(Acc, U, [V | lists:nth(U, Acc)]),
        NewAcc2 = lists:update(NewAcc, V, [U | lists:nth(V, NewAcc)]),
        NewAcc2
    end, Adj, Edges).

% Helper to update an element at a specific index in a list
lists:update(List, Index, Element) ->
    lists:nth(Index, List) =:= _ ->
        lists:replace(List, Index, Element);
    List.

calculate_subset_info(Nums, N, Mask) ->
    Sum = 0,
    SubsetNodes = [],
    
    % Iterate from 0 to N-1
    {TotalSum, Nodes} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        {CurrentSum, CurrentNodes} = Acc,
        
        if (Mask land (1 land<<I)) /= 0 ->
            NewSum = CurrentSum + Nums[I],
            NewNodes = [I | CurrentNodes],
            {NewSum, NewNodes}
        else
            {CurrentSum, CurrentNodes}
        end
    end, {0, []}, 0..N-1),
    
    {TotalSum, lists:reverse(Nodes)}. % Nodes are collected in reverse order

check_connectivity(Adj, Mask, SubsetNodes, N) ->
    case SubsetNodes of
        [] -> false; % Should not happen for mask >= 1
        [StartNode|_] ->
            % Perform BFS/DFS starting from StartNode, only traversing nodes in Mask
            {VisitedCount, _} = bfs(Adj, Mask, StartNode, SubsetNodes, N),
            
            % Connected if we visited all nodes in the subset
            VisitedCount == length(SubsetNodes)
    end.

% BFS implementation
bfs(Adj, Mask, StartNode, SubsetNodes, N) ->
    % Queue: list of nodes to visit
    Queue = [StartNode],
    % Visited: set of nodes already visited (represented by a boolean array or list for simplicity/speed in this context)
    Visited = lists:replicate(N, false),
    lists:replace(Visited, StartNode, true),
    
    {Count, FinalVisited} = bfs_loop(Adj, Mask, Queue, Visited, 0),
    {Count, FinalVisited}.

bfs_loop(Adj, Mask, Queue, Visited, Count) ->
    case Queue of
        [] ->
            {Count, Visited};
        [U | TailQueue] ->
            % Process node U
            Neighbors = Adj[U],
            
            {NewQueue, NewVisited, NewCount} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) ->

                if (Mask land (1 land<<V)) /= 0 and not CurrentVisited[V] ->

                    NewQueue = [V | CurrentQueue],

-module(solver_module).

solve(Nums, Edges) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    Adj = build_adjacency_list(N, Edges),
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate through all masks from 1 to 2^N - 1
    MaxMask = 1 endof(2, N),
    
    do {
        Mask = Mask + 1,
        if Mask > MaxMask ->
            break
        end;
        
        % 1. Check the sum of node values (Parity check)
        {Sum, SubsetNodes} = calculate_subset_info(Nums, N, Mask),
        
        if Sum rem 2 == 0 ->
            % Sum is even, check connectivity
            IsConnected = check_connectivity(Adj, Mask, SubsetNodes, N),
            
            if IsConnected ->
                Count = Count + 1
            end;
        end;
    }, Mask = 1, Count = 0.

build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    % Initialize adjacency list: a list of N empty lists
    Adj = lists:replicate(N, []),
    
    % Fold over edges to build the adjacency list
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, [U, V]) ->
        % Add V to U's neighbors and U to V's neighbors
        NewAcc = lists:update(Acc, U, [V | lists:nth(U, Acc)]),
        NewAcc2 = lists:update(NewAcc, V, [U | lists:nth(V, NewAcc)]),
        NewAcc2
    end, Adj, Edges).

% Helper to update an element at a specific index in a list
lists:update(List, Index, Element) ->
    lists:nth(Index, List) =:= _ ->
        lists:replace(List, Index, Element);
    List.

calculate_subset_info(Nums, N, Mask) ->
    Sum = 0,
    SubsetNodes = [],
    
    % Iterate from 0 to N-1
    {TotalSum, Nodes} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        {CurrentSum, CurrentNodes} = Acc,
        
        if (Mask land (1 land<<I)) /= 0 ->
            NewSum = CurrentSum + Nums[I],
            NewNodes = [I | CurrentNodes],
            {NewSum, NewNodes}
        else
            {CurrentSum, CurrentNodes}
        end
    end, {0, []}, 0..N-1),
    
    {TotalSum, lists:reverse(Nodes)}. % Nodes are collected in reverse order

check_connectivity(Adj, Mask, SubsetNodes, N) ->
    case SubsetNodes of
        [] -> false; % Should not happen for mask >= 1
        [StartNode|_] ->
            % Perform BFS/DFS starting from StartNode, only traversing nodes in Mask
            {VisitedCount, _} = bfs(Adj, Mask, StartNode, SubsetNodes, N),
            
            % Connected if we visited all nodes in the subset
            VisitedCount == length(SubsetNodes)
    end.

% BFS implementation
bfs(Adj, Mask, StartNode, SubsetNodes, N) ->
    % Queue: list of nodes to visit
    Queue = [StartNode],
    % Visited: set of nodes already visited (represented by a boolean array or list for simplicity/speed in this context)
    Visited = lists:replicate(N, false),
    lists:replace(Visited, StartNode, true),
    
    {Count, FinalVisited} = bfs_loop(Adj, Mask, Queue, Visited, 0),
    {Count, FinalVisited}.

bfs_loop(Adj, Mask, Queue, Visited, Count) ->
    case Queue of
        [] ->
            {Count, Visited};
        [U | TailQueue] ->
            % Process node U
            Neighbors = Adj[U],
            
            {NewQueue, NewVisited, NewCount} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) ->
                {CurrentQueue, CurrentVisited, CurrentCount} = Acc,
                
                % Check if V is in subset (Mask) AND not visited
                if (Mask land (1 land<<V)) /= 0 and not CurrentVisited[V] ->
                    NewVisited = lists:replace(CurrentVisited, V, true),
                    NewQueue = [V | CurrentQueue],
                    {NewQueue, NewVisited, CurrentCount + 1}
                else
                    {CurrentQueue, CurrentVisited, CurrentCount}
                end
            end, {TailQueue, Visited, Count}, Neighbors),
            
            bfs_loop(Adj, Mask, NewQueue, NewVisited, NewCount).
```

-export([solve/2]).

%% solve(nums, edges) -> integer()
%% nums: list of integers (0 or 1) representing node values
%% edges: list of lists, where each inner list [u, v] is an edge
solve(Nums, Edges) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    Adj = build_adjacency_list(N, Edges),
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate through all non-empty subsets (masks from 1 to 2^N - 1)
    % 1 << N is 2^N
    do_for(1, 2^N - 1, Mask,
        fun() ->
            % 1. Check the sum of node values (Parity check)
            Sum = calculate_subset_sum(Nums, Mask, N),
            
            if Sum rem 2 /= 0 ->
                % Sum is odd, skip
                true
            ; true ->
                % Sum is even, check connectivity
                SubsetNodes = get_subset_nodes(Mask, N),
                
                case SubsetNodes of
                    [] -> % Should not happen as mask >= 1
                        false
                    [StartNode | _] ->
                        IsConnected = is_connected(Adj, Mask, N, StartNode, SubsetNodes),
                        
                        if IsConnected ->
                            Count = Count + 1
                        ; true ->
                            true
                        end
                end
            end
        end
    ),
    Count.

%% --- Helper Functions ---

% Helper for iteration (similar to for loop in JS)
do_for(Start, End, Pred) ->
    fun() ->
        if Start > End ->
            ok
        ; true ->
            Pred(),
            do_for(Start + 1, End, Pred)
        end
    end.

% Builds adjacency list representation of the graph
build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    %% Initialize N empty lists
    Lists = lists:duplicate(N, []),
    
    %% Fold over edges to populate the lists
    lists:foldl(fun(Adj, [U, V]) ->
        lists:replace(Adj, U, lists:append(lists:nth(U, Adj), [V])),
        lists:replace(Adj, V, lists:append(lists:nth(V, Adj), [U]))
    end, Lists, Edges).

% Calculates the sum of nums[i] for all i present in the mask
calculate_subset_sum(Nums, Mask, N) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        if (Mask div (2^I) mod 2) == 1 ->
            Acc + Nums[I]
        ; true ->
            Acc
        end
    end, 0, lists:seq(0, N - 1)).

% Extracts the list of nodes present in the mask
get_subset_nodes(Mask, N) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        if (Mask div (2^I) mod 2) == 1 ->
            [I | Acc]
        ; true ->
            Acc
        end
    end, [], lists:seq(0, N - 1)).

% Checks if the induced subgraph defined by Mask is connected, starting from StartNode
is_connected(Adj, Mask, N, StartNode, SubsetNodes) ->
    % BFS implementation
    
    % Queue: [StartNode]
    Queue = [StartNode],
    
    % Visited set/list: [StartNode]
    Visited = [StartNode],
    
    % Total expected nodes in the subset
    ExpectedCount = length(SubsetNodes),

    bfs(Adj, Mask, N, Queue, Visited, ExpectedCount) ->

    ; false.

    false. % Queue is empty, not all nodes were visited

    % Check if we found all nodes
    if length(Visited) == ExpectedCount ->
        true
    ; true ->
        % Explore neighbors of U
        Neighbors = Adj[U],
        
        % Filter neighbors: must be in mask, must not be visited
        NewNeighbors = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) ->
            if (Mask div (2^V) mod 2) == 1 and not(lists:member(V, Visited)) ->
                [V | Acc]
            ; true ->
                Acc
            end

        % Update queue and visited list
        NewQueue = RestQueue ++ NewNeighbors,
        NewVisited = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, V) -> [V | Acc] end, Visited, NewNeighbors),
        
        bfs(Adj, Mask, N, NewQueue, NewVisited, ExpectedCount)
    end.

% Helper functions for list manipulation (since Erlang doesn't have direct array/set types easily)

lists:seq(Start, End) ->

lists:replace(List, Index, NewValue) ->

% Custom list insertion at index
lists:insert_at(Index, Value, List) ->
    lists:take(Index, List) ++ [Value] ++ lists:drop(Index, List).

% Custom list taking elements
lists:take(Index, List) ->
    lists:take(Index, List).

% Custom list dropping elements
lists:drop(Index, List) ->
    lists:drop(Index, List).
